other_observers: None; Solomon (Sol) Miller, a half mile away from where I was. He is a birder and a Ranger at Fossil Creek Reservoir. I called him and was going to tell him that I had found Brown Pelican but he was already seeing it when he got my call.
specific_location: Flying above Fossil Creek Reservoir, going from the West to the East along Fossil Creek Reservoir and finally disappeared when it flew far enough East that I couldn't see it anymore.
description: A very brown bird, slightly smaller than the American White Pelicans it was flying with, had white on the belly and a white on the head, brown on the front and back of the neck, back of the head, back and wings, the tips of the under parts of the wings were slightly darker than the rest of the wing.Behaviors: Flying (circling) above Fossil Creek Reservoir I never saw it on the ground or on the water. It flew, with 8 American White Pelicans, towards the East (Windsor).Call: I didn't hear any sound.Plumage: Alternate 2nd or 3rd year
